div.head: The Minister in Yugoslavia (Lane) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 291. Reference Bucharest’s 319, June 28, 7 p.m.Not printed. A reliable source has informed us that Russia has already requested a place on the commission for the control of the mouths of the DanubeThe European Commission of the Danube. and on the International Danube Commission. My informant is of the opinion that the Russian request will be sponsored by the British with the hope that the Russian vote can be counted on to conflict with those of the Axis Powers.
The British MinisterSir Ronald I. Campbell. while noncommittal on the subject admitted that it was now to be expected that Russia should be represented on both commissions.
The Italian representative on the Danube Commission who resides in Belgrade denies that any Russian request has been made, but admits without enthusiasm that such a move is now to be expected.
